description_long: This object is a vintage-style television with a boxy, rectangular body finished in a muted tan color. The front features a recessed, dark screen bordered by a thick, wooden-like frame, and two prominent round control knobs below the screen, likely for channel and volume adjustment. On top, two thin metal antennas extend upward from a rounded, dome-like base, evoking classic analog TV designs. The rear of the television protrudes outward in a trapezoidal shape, typical of cathode ray tube (CRT) technology, and is colored in a contrasting dark gray. The sides and top are smooth and unadorned, except for the antenna mount. This television would be found in urban settings such as old apartments, garages, or as discarded electronics on the street.
description: A retro, box-shaped television with a tan body, wooden-framed screen, two control knobs, and metal antennas on top. The back features a protruding, dark gray CRT housing.
description_view_0: Front view shows the screen with a wooden frame, two round control knobs, and the base of the antennas.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the boxy tan body, the dome-shaped antenna base, and the protruding CRT back.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the dark gray, angular CRT housing and the top-mounted antennas.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors the other side, showing the tan body and the CRT back, with the antenna base visible on top.
attribute_bezel_design: thick, brown, wooden-like bezel with a recessed screen
attribute_control_button_layout: two round knobs on the lower front panel
